name: output-format-enforcer
prompt:
Add an output format enforcer for the execute phase.

In agentrail/run/output_enforcer.py expose:
- Accepted and Rejected result types. enforce returns an instance of one of these (callers check via isinstance). A Rejected carries a non-empty .reason string explaining the rejection; Accepted carries no required reason.
- enforce(content, is_new_or_rename=False) -> Accepted | Rejected
- all_changes_new_or_rename(porcelain_str) -> bool (given `git status --porcelain` text, are all changed paths new or renames?)
- push_format_rejection_event(workdir, run_id, kind, reason) -> bool (non-fatal; emits a session event)

Contract — enforce: when the agent edits an existing file it must return a unified diff, not a full-file rewrite. enforce treats the presence of a unified-diff hunk header anywhere in the content as a valid diff and returns Accepted. A hunk header is any line of the form '@@ ... @@' — this includes the comma form '@@ -1,5 +1,6 @@', the no-comma form '@@ -1 +1 @@', a header carrying a trailing section label '@@ -10,3 +10,4 @@ def my_function():', multiple hunks in one payload, and a hunk header embedded anywhere inside larger prose/content. With is_new_or_rename=True, enforce returns Accepted for ANY content, including empty content and large multi-hundred-line content with no diff markers. With is_new_or_rename=False (the default — editing an existing file), content that contains no '@@' hunk header is Rejected; this explicitly includes empty content (clearing a file without a diff) and large plain text with no '@@'. Every Rejected.reason is a non-empty string and must literally contain the substring '@@' to point the agent at the expected hunk-header marker.

Contract — all_changes_new_or_rename: parse `git status --porcelain` lines. Return True only when there is at least one entry AND every entry is an add ('A '), untracked ('??'), or rename ('R '/'R  old -> new'). Return False when any entry is a modified-existing-file (e.g. ' M existing.py'), when the set mixes an add/rename with such a modification, and when the input is empty or whitespace-only.

Contract — push_format_rejection_event: POST a single session event and return a bool; it must NEVER raise. The posted body is a JSON list containing exactly one event object with these fields: session_id equal to the run_id argument, a top-level kind equal to the kind argument, and an action object whose type is the literal string 'output_format_rejected', whose phase equals the kind argument, and which includes the reason. It returns True only when the server accepts the event with HTTP status 202; it returns False (without raising) when the workdir is not linked to a server (no .agentrail/server.json and no server env vars) or when the network call errors. The server link is read from .agentrail/server.json (keys base_url, api_key, repository_id).
